sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.34 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a topological group and$S={G_0 G_n=G}$a subnormal series of closed subgroups. The of$S$is the cardinality of the set of infinite factors$G_i/G_{i-1}$. The of$G$is the supremum of the infinite lengths taken over all such series of$G$. It is known that if$G$is a pronilpotent group with finite virtual length then every closed subnormal subgroup is topologically finitely generated (N.\,Gaviol...
